author: @corydwagner
text:
If you have a Klipper-based printer, it's super easy to connect the camera to the printer and install timelapse software. The advantage is that the printer knows when it's about to change layers, so you get closer to the same location every time. That really reduces jerkiness.

For Marlin-based printers, you can't beat the Beagle from Mintion. It centers the bed and moves the print head to the left for just a second after every layer. As a result, your model just builds as if from nowhere!

The Beagle also gives you lots of remote monitoring and management functionality that isn't available out-of-the-box with Marlin. Best $65 accessory I bought for my Anycubic Kobra.

It doesn't work with Klipper, however.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: nice tips mate - cheers

author: @dondywondy
text:
Great video! Good information to know.
In the time lapse video it seems the camera is zooming in and out slightly from time to time; do you have any idea what's causing that to occur?

replies.author: @corydwagner
replies.text: I think it's more that the bed is moving and is not at the same position in every capture.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: the bed movement mate - i forgot to turn off auto focus!

author: @cmdrredhawk
text:
You can use cura to insert G code to move fhe print head ro the side and move the bed Z height to 0 for fhe picture to be taken. 

Makes the model appear to form out of nothing

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: you can indeed and it is pretty impressive but you need to link that with an actuator to operate the camera at the right time - it's easy enough a limit switch and 220 ohm resistor will do it if you use  mobile phone

author: @kellyjones71
text:
Hey Great Idea. Thanks for the explanation. You could make it less jerky by reducing the number of pics. Perhaps only even or odd numbered pics. I would think that any loss of continuity between frames would be so miniscule that you wouldn't notice.

Wait a minute less pics would be more jerkiness. Yes, I think so. Hmmm..perhaps reducing the pics and tinkering with frame rates and timing? You always get me thinking of stuff with your vids. Love Em Mate.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it's the thinking that matters mate - cheers

author: @arb261268
text: I have to say I prefer sped up video over Timelapse for something that is constantly moving, it’s a bit too jerky for me. But I have to say that did look a very easy way to create a Timelapse.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: 12 hours is a long video mate

replies.author: @arb261268
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering very true and that would be a lot more storage too. I just prefer the aesthetics of sped up vid, very beefy computer with lots and lots of storage. Much less so for Timelapse but that’s the compromise. As always though an informative and entertaining piece from yourself.
